meta_title: Roofing in Sunrise, FL | Premier Impact Windows
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Sunrise, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, A+ BBB, financing from $87/mo. Free estimate!
h1: Expert Roof Replacement & Repair Services for Sunrise, Florida Homeowners
intro:
Sunrise, Florida sits at the heart of Broward County — a city of nearly 100,000 residents spread across thriving communities like Sunrise Lakes, Sawgrass, Spring Lake, Welleby, and Nob Hill. It's a city defined by its suburban pride, its proximity to Sawgrass Mills Mall and the BB&T Center, and the kind of tight-knit neighborhood culture that makes homeownership here genuinely rewarding. But Sunrise is also a city where the roof over your head is tested every single season. Situated roughly 10 miles inland from the Atlantic coastline, Sunrise sits in a designated 170 mph wind zone — meaning every roofing system must be engineered to perform under extraordinary pressure. When storm season arrives, your roof is the single most important barrier between your family and the elements, and in Sunrise, that's not a line worth cutting corners on.

With approximately 50% of homes in Sunrise built before 1994 — before modern hurricane-resistant building codes were put into place — a significant portion of the housing stock in ZIP codes like 33313, 33321, 33322, 33323, 33325, and 33351 is working with aging roofing systems that were never designed to meet today's Florida Building Code standards. For homeowners with average property values hovering around $350,000, a failing or outdated roof isn't just a structural risk — it's a financial liability. Whether your home is a mid-century ranch in Welleby or a newer construction near the Sawgrass Expressway corridor, protecting that investment starts at the rooftop.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by delivering world-class roofing solutions to homeowners throughout Broward County — and Sunrise is no exception. With over 60 years of combined installation experience, a 100% in-house crew, and an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, we bring the expertise, materials, and accountability that Sunrise homeowners deserve. From full roof replacements using CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles to stunning West Lake concrete and clay tile systems, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing ensures every roof we install is rated for Florida's harshest conditions, permitted through the City of Sunrise Building Division, and built to protect your home for decades to come.

section_why.h2: Why Sunrise Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Needs
section_why.body:
Choosing the right roofing contractor in Sunrise isn't just about getting the lowest quote — it's about partnering with a team that understands Broward County's unique climate demands, local permitting requirements, and the specific challenges facing homes in this region. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings more than six decades of combined installation experience to every project, and unlike many contractors who rely on subcontractors, our entire roofing crew is 100% in-house. That means consistent quality, direct accountability, and a team that shows up to your home with a personal stake in the work they produce.

We've completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and our track record in communities throughout Sunrise — from the lakeside homes of Sunrise Lakes to the established streets of Nob Hill — speaks for itself. Our team pulls permits directly through the City of Sunrise Building Division, handles every inspection, and ensures your roofing project is fully compliant with current Florida Building Code requirements. You never have to chase down a permit or wonder if the work was done to code. We handle every detail from the initial estimate to the final inspection sign-off.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida, which matters enormously when you're trusting a contractor to work on what is arguably the most critical structural component of your home. We carry all required liability and workers' compensation coverage, so you're protected from any unexpected complications. And because we believe that quality roofing should be accessible to every Sunrise homeowner regardless of budget, we offer flexible financing options starting from just $87 per month with $0 down options available. Whether you're replacing a decades-old shingle roof or upgrading to a premium tile system, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes it possible without financial strain.

Our A+ BBB rating isn't just a badge — it reflects thousands of interactions with real Broward County homeowners who expected excellence and received it. We take pride in clear communication, honest timelines, and roofing systems that don't just look beautiful but perform when it matters most. When the next storm tests the rooftops of Sunrise, we want every home we've worked on to stand strong.

section_history.h2: Sunrise Has a Storm Story — And Your Roof Has to Be Ready for the Next Chapter
section_history.body:
Sunrise has weathered nine named storms since 1990, and the scars left by three major hurricanes have shaped how this community thinks about home protection. Hurricane Andrew made landfall in 1992 as one of the most destructive storms in American history, and while its full fury was directed at Miami-Dade County, Broward communities like Sunrise experienced significant wind damage that exposed just how vulnerable aging roofing systems truly were. Andrew was a wake-up call that directly led to the sweeping revisions in Florida's building codes — revisions that many pre-1994 homes in Sunrise were never subject to during their original construction.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered another brutal lesson to Sunrise residents. Wilma crossed the Florida peninsula as a Category 3 storm and produced some of the strongest sustained winds Broward County had seen in decades. Neighborhoods across Sunrise — from Spring Lake to Sawgrass — saw widespread roof damage, including thousands of shingles torn away, underlayment failures, and tile systems that simply weren't secured adequately. Many homeowners discovered too late that their roofs had passed visual inspections but lacked the structural integrity needed to survive a major storm. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced the same message, with wind-driven rain and gusts that peeled back poorly installed or aged roofing systems across the county.

These storms underscore a critical truth for Sunrise homeowners: a roof that looks fine from the street may be one major hurricane away from catastrophic failure. If your home was built before 1994 — and roughly half of Sunrise's housing stock was — there's a very real possibility that your current roofing system doesn't meet today's code requirements for a 170 mph wind zone. Investing in a properly installed, hurricane-rated roofing system isn't alarmism; it's the most rational response to a storm history that has proven, repeatedly, that Sunrise is not immune to nature's worst.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Sunrise and Broward County
section_code.body:
Sunrise falls under the jurisdiction of Broward County and is subject to the Florida Building Code (FBC), which is widely regarded as one of the most stringent residential construction standards in the United States. While Sunrise is not within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation typically reserved for Miami-Dade and portions of Broward closer to the coast — the city still requires that all roofing products and installation methods meet hurricane-rated standards appropriate for its 170 mph design wind speed. This means every shingle, tile, fastener, and underlayment system installed in Sunrise must be tested and approved for high-wind performance under the current edition of the FBC.

For roofing projects in Sunrise, all work requires a permit pulled through the City of Sunrise Building Division. This is not optional, and any contractor who suggests bypassing the permit process — even for seemingly straightforward repairs — is putting your home, your insurance coverage, and your future resale value at risk. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the permitting process from start to finish, submitting documentation, scheduling required inspections, and ensuring every phase of the installation is reviewed by a city inspector before the project is closed out. This protects you legally, financially, and structurally.

The FBC also mandates specific requirements for roofing underlayment, nail patterns, and attachment methods in wind zones like Sunrise's. Architectural shingles must meet ASTM D3161 Class F or ASTM D7158 Class H wind resistance testing, and tile systems must be mechanically fastened rather than simply set in mortar. These aren't just bureaucratic checkboxes — they're the reason hurricane-rated roofs survive while non-compliant ones fail. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installation teams are trained and certified to meet these exacting standards, ensuring that every roof we install in Sunrise neighborhoods like Welleby, Sawgrass, and Nob Hill is built to the letter of the law and the spirit of storm protection.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Series Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Built for Sunrise
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we don't work with just any roofing material — we've carefully selected product lines that combine proven hurricane performance, aesthetic versatility, and long-term durability for South Florida's demanding climate. For homeowners in Sunrise who prefer the traditional look of asphalt shingles, we install CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles, one of the most respected product lines in the residential roofing industry. The Landmark Series features a dual-layered design that provides superior dimensional appearance while delivering Class A fire resistance and wind resistance ratings up to 130 mph with standard installation — and up to 150 mph with enhanced nailing patterns appropriate for Broward County's wind zone requirements. CertainTeed backs the Landmark Series with a robust warranty program, including lifetime limited coverage and a 10-year SureStart protection period that covers 100% of repair and replacement costs.

For homeowners who want to elevate the curb appeal and resale value of their Sunrise property, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also installs West Lake concrete and clay tile roofing systems. Tile roofing has been the signature look of South Florida architecture for generations, and for good reason — it's extraordinarily durable, naturally resistant to the heat and humidity of a Broward County summer, and virtually impervious to the UV degradation that shortens the lifespan of other materials. West Lake's concrete and clay tile products are manufactured to meet Florida's high-wind product approval requirements, and when properly installed with mechanical fasteners and approved underlayment systems, they deliver exceptional performance in storm conditions.

Both product lines are available in a wide range of colors and profiles, allowing Sunrise homeowners in communities ranging from the lakeside residences of Sunrise Lakes to the family homes of Spring Lake to choose a roofing system that complements their home's architecture. Whether you're drawn to the clean lines of a charcoal architectural shingle or the warm tones of a Spanish S-tile in clay, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's product consultants will help you find the right system for your home, your budget, and your storm protection goals.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Sunrise, FL — and How Can You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions Sunrise homeowners ask when they begin exploring a roof replacement is simply: what is this going to cost me? The honest answer is that pricing varies based on the size of your home, the roofing system you select, the condition of your existing deck, and whether any structural repairs are needed. For a typical Sunrise home — valued around the city's average of $350,000 and ranging from 1,500 to 2,500 square feet — a full roof replacement using CertainTeed Landmark Series architectural shingles typically falls in the range of $12,000 to $20,000. A West Lake tile installation, which requires more labor-intensive installation and heavier structural consideration, generally ranges from $18,000 to $30,000 or more depending on tile profile and home complexity.

While these numbers may seem significant, it's important to frame them in the context of return on investment. A new roof is one of the highest-ROI home improvements available, with national studies consistently showing 60–70% cost recoupment at resale — and in a hot South Florida real estate market like Sunrise, where buyers are acutely aware of storm risk and insurance costs, a new hurricane-rated roof can be a decisive selling point. Beyond resale, a properly documented roof replacement can yield immediate insurance premium reductions that offset financing costs year after year.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options specifically designed to make roof replacement accessible for middle-income Sunrise homeowners. With approved credit, qualified customers can get started with $0 down and payments starting as low as $87 per month. We work with multiple lending partners to match you with terms that fit your monthly budget, so you never have to delay a critical roof replacement because of upfront cost concerns. Our team provides transparent, itemized estimates with no hidden fees — and we're happy to walk you through every line item so you fully understand what you're paying for and why.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Sunrise Can Lower Your Homeowners Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
In Florida's homeowners insurance market — which has seen dramatic premium increases across Broward County over the past several years — a new hurricane-rated roof is one of the most powerful tools a Sunrise homeowner has to control insurance costs. Florida insurers are required to offer discounts for homes that meet specific wind mitigation standards, and a new roof installed to current Florida Building Code requirements is one of the most significant contributors to a favorable wind mitigation inspection report. Depending on your insurer and the specifics of your roof system, Sunrise homeowners have reported annual premium savings ranging from $500 to over $2,000 after a qualifying roof replacement — savings that, compounded over the life of the roof, can substantially offset the total investment.

The wind mitigation credit system in Florida is tied to specific construction features that are documented on a standardized OIR-B1-1802 form, completed by a licensed inspector. Key factors include roof deck attachment method, roof-to-wall connection type, roof covering type, and whether the roof meets the opening protection requirements appropriate for your wind zone. When Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs a new CertainTeed or West Lake roof system, we ensure every element of the installation is documented properly and aligned with the criteria that generate the maximum available credits under Florida's wind mitigation program.

For Sunrise homeowners in ZIP codes like 33322 or 33325, where many homes are older and may currently carry actuarially adjusted rates that reflect aging roof systems, the insurance math can be compelling. Some carriers — including Citizens Property Insurance and major private market insurers operating in Broward County — have become increasingly strict about insuring homes with roofs over 15 to 20 years old. In some cases, a dated roof can make a home uninsurable without replacement. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing helps you get ahead of that problem, ensuring your Sunrise home remains properly insured, adequately protected, and financially positioned for long-term stability.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Sunrise home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: The best way to determine the right course of action is with a professional inspection. In Sunrise, where roughly half of homes were built before 1994 and have been exposed to multiple major hurricanes, many aging roofs show damage that isn't visible from the ground — including compromised underlayment, deteriorated fasteners, and cracked or lifting tile. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections and will give you an honest assessment of whether repair or full replacement makes more sense for your specific roof, age, and condition.

faqs.q: What are the financing options for a roof replacement in Sunrise, FL?
faqs.a: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting from as little as $87 per month with $0 down for qualified applicants. We work with multiple lending partners to provide competitive terms suited to a range of credit profiles and budgets. For a typical Sunrise home requiring a full roof replacement — which can range from $12,000 to $30,000 depending on material and size — financing makes it possible to protect your home now and spread the cost over manageable monthly payments without draining savings.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for roof replacement in Sunrise, and who handles it?
faqs.a: Yes, all roofing replacements in Sunrise require a permit pulled through the City of Sunrise Building Division. This is required by the Florida Building Code and is not something any reputable contractor should bypass. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — we submit the documentation, coordinate inspections, and ensure your project receives a proper sign-off from the city. This protects your warranty, your insurance coverage, and your home's resale value.

faqs.q: How long does a full roof replacement take in Sunrise?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Sunrise, a full shingle roof replacement can typically be completed in one to two days once materials are delivered and the permit is approved. Tile roof replacements may take two to four days depending on the size and complexity of the roof. The permitting process through the City of Sunrise Building Division typically adds one to two weeks to the overall timeline before installation begins. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will walk you through every stage of the schedule so you always know what to expect.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowners insurance in Sunrise?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation discounts for homes with qualifying roof systems, and a new hurricane-rated roof installed to current Florida Building Code standards is one of the most impactful factors on a wind mitigation inspection report. Sunrise homeowners have reported annual insurance savings of $500 to over $2,000 after a qualifying replacement. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs roofing systems that are specifically aligned with Florida's wind mitigation credit criteria to help you maximize those savings.

cta_headline: Sunrise Homeowners: Get a Free Roofing Estimate from The Kings of Service
cta_body: Whether your roof is decades old, storm-damaged, or simply due for an upgrade, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to protect your Sunrise home with a hurricane-rated system built for Florida's toughest conditions. Contact us today for your free, no-obligation estimate — and discover why hundreds of Broward County homeowners trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing with their most important investment.
